---
title: "Tools Comparison (Chat): Browser Agent + ChatGPT"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false

```

## Run Metadata

- Harness: `browser-agent`
- Model: `chatgpt`
- Prompt type: `chat`

## Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false

library(dplyr)

df <- readr::read_csv(here::here("data/synthetic/simulated_maternal_health_data.csv"))

df |> glimpse()
```

## Prompt 

```text
I want you to help me analyze a dataset on maternal health.

My goal is to produce a thorough analysis that is statistically sound and lays out all steps of the analysis.

I've pasted below the results of the `glimpse` function from dplyr, which shows you variable names, types, and some example values.

Give me code, labeled by bullet number below to:

1. Help me ensure variable names are standardized and that they're correctly typed in the R dataframe.

2. Summarize the data:
   - Report row count, column count, missingness, and key descriptive summaries.
   - Flag suspicious values or quality concerns.

3. Create key visualizations.
   - Show relationships for:
     - `received_comprehensive_postnatal_care`
     - `distance_to_provider`
     - `insurance`
     - `race_ethnicity`

4. Help me build a count outcome and model it.
   - Define `comorbidity_count` as the row-wise sum of:
     - `obesity`,
     - `multiple_gestation`,
     - `diabetes`,
     - `heart_disease`,
     - `placenta_previa`,
     - `hypertension`,
     - `gest_hypertension`,
     - `preeclampsia`.
   - Give me diagnostics to help me determine if I've picked an appropriate model given the data.
   - Fit the model and report interpretable results.

Constraints:
- Prefer readable, modular, reproducible code that follows R community idioms.
- Report back a summary of what you changed.

Please give me a single codeblock back I can cut and paste back into a quarto notebook.
```

```{r}
# ── 0. Packages ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(janitor)
library(skimr)
library(patchwork)
library(lme4)
library(DHARMa)
library(broom.mixed)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(performance)

# ── 1. Standardize variable names & types ────────────────────────────────────

# Names are already snake_case from readr, but clean_names() will enforce
# consistency (lowercase, no special chars) idempotently.
df <- df |>
  janitor::clean_names()

# Parse ordered factors and binary indicators
income_levels <- c(
  "Less than $25,000",
  "$25,000–$49,999",
  "$50,000–$74,999",
  "$75,000–$99,999",
  "$100,000 or more"
)

edu_levels <- c("less_than_hs", "hs", "some_college", "bachelors", "graduate")

df <- df |>
  mutate(
    # IDs → not numeric predictors
    id          = as.character(id),
    provider_id = as.character(provider_id),

    # Binary outcome
    received_comprehensive_postnatal_care =
      as.integer(received_comprehensive_postnatal_care),

    # Ordered factors
    self_report_income = factor(self_report_income,
                                levels = income_levels, ordered = TRUE),
    edu = factor(edu, levels = edu_levels, ordered = TRUE),

    # Unordered factors
    state          = factor(state),
    race_ethnicity = factor(race_ethnicity),
    insurance      = factor(insurance),
    job_type       = factor(job_type),

    # Binary indicators → integer (already 0/1)
    across(c(obesity, multiple_gestation, diabetes, heart_disease,
             placenta_previa, hypertension, gest_hypertension, preeclampsia),
           as.integer)
  )

cat("── 1. Summary of changes ──\n")
cat("• Cleaned column names via clean_names()\n")
cat("• id, provider_id → character (identifiers, not predictors)\n")
cat("• self_report_income → ordered factor (5 levels)\n")
cat("• edu → ordered factor (5 levels)\n")
cat("• state, race_ethnicity, insurance, job_type → unordered factors\n")
cat("• Binary comorbidity columns → integer\n")
cat("• received_comprehensive_postnatal_care → integer\n\n")

glimpse(df)


# ── 2. Data summary & quality checks ────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n── 2a. Dimensions ──\n")
cat(glue::glue("Rows: {nrow(df)}   Columns: {ncol(df)}"), "\n\n")

cat("── 2b. Skim summary ──\n")
skim(df)

# Missingness report
cat("\n── 2c. Missingness by column ──\n")
miss_tbl <- df |>

  summarise(across(everything(), \(x) sum(is.na(x)))) |>

  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "n_missing") |>
  mutate(pct_missing = n_missing / nrow(df) * 100) |>
  filter(n_missing > 0) |>
  arrange(desc(n_missing))

if (nrow(miss_tbl) == 0) {

  cat("No missing values detected.\n")
} else {
  print(miss_tbl, n = Inf)
}

# Flag suspicious values
cat("\n── 2d. Quality flags ──\n")

# Age range check
cat(glue::glue("Age range: {min(df$age, na.rm=TRUE)}–{max(df$age, na.rm=TRUE)}"), "\n")
n_age_flag <- sum(df$age < 10 | df$age > 55, na.rm = TRUE)
cat(glue::glue("  Ages outside 10–55: {n_age_flag}"), "\n")

# Distance outliers
dist_q <- quantile(df$distance_to_provider, c(0.01, 0.99), na.rm = TRUE)
cat(glue::glue("Distance 1st–99th pctile: {round(dist_q[1],1)}–{round(dist_q[2],1)}"), "\n")
n_dist_flag <- sum(df$distance_to_provider <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)
cat(glue::glue("  Distance ≤ 0: {n_dist_flag}"), "\n")

# Binary columns: anything not 0/1?
binary_cols <- c("obesity", "multiple_gestation", "diabetes", "heart_disease",
                 "placenta_previa", "hypertension", "gest_hypertension",
                 "preeclampsia", "received_comprehensive_postnatal_care")
bad_binary <- df |>
  summarise(across(all_of(binary_cols), \(x) sum(!x %in% c(0L, 1L, NA)))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "n_bad") |>
  filter(n_bad > 0)
if (nrow(bad_binary) == 0) {
  cat("All binary columns contain only 0/1.\n")
} else {
  cat("Non-0/1 values in binary columns:\n")
  print(bad_binary)
}

# Factor levels check
cat("\nFactor level counts:\n")
df |>
  select(where(is.factor)) |>
  walk2(names(df |> select(where(is.factor))),
        \(col, nm) cat(glue::glue("  {nm}: {nlevels(col)} levels"), "\n"))


# ── 3. Key visualizations ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12))

# 3a. Postnatal care rate by race/ethnicity
p1 <- df |>
  group_by(race_ethnicity) |>
  summarise(
    n    = n(),
    rate = mean(received_comprehensive_postnatal_care, na.rm = TRUE),
    se   = sqrt(rate * (1 - rate) / n),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(race_ethnicity = fct_reorder(race_ethnicity, rate)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = race_ethnicity, y = rate)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#3B7EA1", width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rate - 1.96 * se, ymax = rate + 1.96 * se),
                width = 0.2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Postnatal care rate by race/ethnicity",
       x = NULL, y = "Rate of comprehensive postnatal care")

# 3b. Postnatal care by insurance
p2 <- df |>
  group_by(insurance) |>
  summarise(
    n    = n(),
    rate = mean(received_comprehensive_postnatal_care, na.rm = TRUE),
    se   = sqrt(rate * (1 - rate) / n),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(insurance = fct_reorder(insurance, rate)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = insurance, y = rate)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#C4820E", width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rate - 1.96 * se, ymax = rate + 1.96 * se),
                width = 0.2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Postnatal care rate by insurance status",
       x = NULL, y = "Rate of comprehensive postnatal care")

# 3c. Distance to provider — distribution & relationship w/ care
p3 <- df |>
  mutate(care = factor(received_comprehensive_postnatal_care,
                       labels = c("No", "Yes"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = distance_to_provider, fill = care)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(trans = "log1p",
                     breaks = c(0, 1, 5, 10, 25, 50, 100)) +
  labs(title = "Distance to provider by postnatal care receipt",
       x = "Distance (log scale)", y = "Density", fill = "Received care")

# 3d. Postnatal care rate by distance decile and race
p4 <- df |>
  mutate(dist_decile = ntile(distance_to_provider, 10)) |>
  group_by(dist_decile, race_ethnicity) |>
  summarise(rate = mean(received_comprehensive_postnatal_care, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = dist_decile, y = rate, color = race_ethnicity)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  labs(title = "Postnatal care rate by distance decile & race/ethnicity",
       x = "Distance decile", y = "Rate", color = "Race/ethnicity")

(p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Maternal health — key relationships",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")))


# ── 4. Comorbidity count outcome ─────────────────────────────────────────────

# 4a. Create comorbidity count
comorbid_vars <- c("obesity", "multiple_gestation", "diabetes",
                   "heart_disease", "placenta_previa", "hypertension",
                   "gest_hypertension", "preeclampsia")

df <- df |>
  mutate(comorbidity_count = rowSums(pick(all_of(comorbid_vars))))

# 4b. Diagnostics — is Poisson appropriate?
cat("\n── 4b. Comorbidity count diagnostics ──\n")
cat(glue::glue("Mean:     {round(mean(df$comorbidity_count), 3)}"), "\n")
cat(glue::glue("Variance: {round(var(df$comorbidity_count), 3)}"), "\n")
cat(glue::glue("Ratio (var/mean): {round(var(df$comorbidity_count)/mean(df$comorbidity_count), 3)}"), "\n")
cat("  → If ratio ≈ 1 → Poisson ok; if >> 1 → overdispersion → negative binomial\n")
cat("  → If ratio << 1 → underdispersion (common for bounded sums of rare events)\n\n")

# Distribution plot
p_hist <- df |>
  count(comorbidity_count) |>
  mutate(
    obs_prop = n / sum(n),
    pois_prop = dpois(comorbidity_count, lambda = mean(df$comorbidity_count))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = comorbidity_count)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = obs_prop), fill = "#3B7EA1", alpha = 0.7, width = 0.4,
           position = position_nudge(x = -0.2)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = pois_prop), fill = "#C4820E", alpha = 0.7, width = 0.4,
           position = position_nudge(x = 0.2)) +
  labs(title = "Observed (blue) vs. Poisson expected (gold)",
       x = "Comorbidity count", y = "Proportion") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_hist)

# 4c. Fit Poisson GLMM (provider as random intercept)
cat("── 4c. Fitting Poisson GLMM ──\n")
cat("Model: comorbidity_count ~ age + race_ethnicity + insurance + edu +\n")
cat("       distance_to_provider + self_report_income + (1 | provider_id)\n\n")

m_pois <- glmer(
  comorbidity_count ~ age + race_ethnicity + insurance + edu +
    scale(distance_to_provider) + self_report_income +
    (1 | provider_id),
  data    = df,
  family  = poisson(link = "log"),
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 1e5))
)

# DHARMa residual diagnostics
cat("── DHARMa residual diagnostics ──\n")
sim_res <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = m_pois, n = 500, plot = TRUE)

# Overdispersion test
cat("\n── Overdispersion test ──\n")
testDispersion(sim_res)

# If overdispersed, refit with negative binomial
disp_ratio <- var(df$comorbidity_count) / mean(df$comorbidity_count)
if (disp_ratio > 1.25) {
  cat("\n── Variance/mean ratio > 1.25 — refitting with negative binomial ──\n")
  m_nb <- glmer.nb(
    comorbidity_count ~ age + race_ethnicity + insurance + edu +
      scale(distance_to_provider) + self_report_income +
      (1 | provider_id),
    data    = df,
    control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 1e5))
  )

  cat("\n── NB model DHARMa diagnostics ──\n")
  sim_res_nb <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = m_nb, n = 500, plot = TRUE)
  testDispersion(sim_res_nb)

  final_model <- m_nb
  cat("\n→ Using negative binomial model as final model.\n")
} else {
  final_model <- m_pois
  cat("\n→ Poisson model fits adequately — using as final model.\n")
}

# 4d. Results — rate ratios with 95% CIs
cat("\n── 4d. Model results (exponentiated = rate ratios) ──\n")
results <- tidy(final_model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE,
                effects = "fixed") |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), \(x) round(x, 3)))

print(results, n = Inf)

cat("\n── Random effects ──\n")
print(VarCorr(final_model))

cat("\n── Model summary ──\n")
summary(final_model)
```
